title: Remove a limit or home sensor
summary: Safely remove a limit or home sensor from the tipper by identifying the sensor and axis, applying lockout/tagout, disconnecting the sensor from the correct connection point, removing the retaining screw, and freeing the routed cable for replacement.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a limit sensor or home sensor on the tipper must be removed as part of replacement service.
not_for: Installing a new limit or home sensor; the provided source packet only covers removal guidance.
Determining exact sensor physical location; the candidate notes that location details are referenced on the previous page and are not included in this section.
Recovering from cable routing problems or wire-tie constraints beyond the described removal actions.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, access the applicable connection point, remove the retaining hardware, and free the sensor cable from the machine routing.

safety_notes: Use LOTO before performing the removal.
Access may be required near the A-axis motor or inside the control box depending on sensor axis.
access_or_tools_needed: Replacement part: limit sensor or home sensor
Access to PCA near the A-axis motor for A-axis sensors
Access to the CLINK controller inside the control box for Z-axis sensors

access_required: Access to PCA near the A-axis motor for A-axis sensors
Access to the CLINK controller inside the control box for Z-axis sensors
system_preconditions: Identify whether the sensor being removed is a home sensor or a limit sensor.
Identify whether the sensor is on the A-axis or Z-axis before disconnecting it.
Tipper is placed in lockout/tagout state before removal begins.

visual_references.description: CLINK controller identification for Z-axis sensor connection point inside the control box.
Tip PCA identification for A-axis sensor connection area.
Tip or A-axis cable routing reference useful when freeing routed cable.
Tip or A-axis cable service figure that may help identify cable path context.
Referenced in the candidate inline image note, but only loosely related clamp hardware context is available in this packet.

steps.title: Gather required PPE, tools, and replacement part
steps.purpose: Ensure the technician has the required items before beginning sensor removal.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement limit sensor or home sensor, safety glasses, gloves, a 1.5-mm hex wrench, a tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties before starting the removal procedure.
steps.expected_result: All required PPE, tools, and materials are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses, gloves, 1.5-mm hex wrench, wire-tie cutting tool, wire ties, and the replacement sensor are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: Required PPE, tools, or replacement part are missing.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the replacement limit sensor or home sensor, safety glasses, gloves, a 1.5-mm hex wrench, a tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required PPE is unavailable.
Required tools or replacement part are unavailable.

source_evidence.text: Gather the replacement limit sensor or home sensor, safety glasses, gloves, a 1.5-mm hex wrench, a tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties.
LOTO access for the tipper; Replacement part: limit sensor or home sensor; PPE: safety glasses; PPE: gloves; 1.5-mm hex wrench; Tool to cut wire ties; Wire ties.

steps.title: Identify sensor type and axis
steps.purpose: Determine the correct removal path and connection point based on sensor type and axis.
steps.instruction: Identify whether the sensor is a home sensor or limit sensor and whether it is on the A-axis or Z-axis. Use the source distinctions that home sensors are normally open (NO), limit sensors are normally closed (NC), Z-axis sensors have long cables greater than 6 ft, and A-axis sensors have shorter cables less than 2 ft.
steps.expected_result: The technician knows the sensor type and axis and can proceed to the correct connection point.
steps.healthy_condition: Sensor type and axis are clearly identified before disconnecting any cable.
steps.failure_condition: Sensor type or axis cannot be confidently identified from the available source information.

actions.label: Confirm sensor type
actions.exact_action: Identify the sensor as a home sensor (normally open) or limit sensor (normally closed).

actions.label: Confirm axis by cable length
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the sensor is Z-axis with a long cable (>6 ft) or A-axis with a shorter cable (<2 ft).

stop_or_escalate_if: The sensor type cannot be determined.
The axis cannot be determined.
The technician cannot confirm the correct connection point from the available source.

source_evidence.text: Identify the sensor type and axis details before removal: home sensors are normally open (NO), limit sensors are normally closed (NC), Z-axis sensors have long cables (>6 ft), and A-axis sensors have shorter cables (<2 ft).
Sensor location details are referenced on the previous page and are not included in this section.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before performing sensor removal.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the manual's Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before disconnecting or removing the sensor.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in a lockout/tagout state and safe for maintenance work.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been applied in accordance with the referenced procedure before any removal work continues.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not locked out and tagged out.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the manual's "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: LOTO cannot be applied.
Any removal work would proceed without LOTO.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the manual's "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure.
Use LOTO before performing the removal.

steps.title: Unplug the sensor from the correct connection point
steps.purpose: Disconnect the sensor electrically before removing it from its mounting and routing path.
steps.instruction: Unplug the sensor from its connection point. For an A-axis sensor, unplug it from the printed circuit assembly (PCA) near the A-axis motor. For a Z-axis sensor, unplug it from the CLINK controller inside the control box.
steps.expected_result: The sensor cable is electrically disconnected from the machine.
steps.healthy_condition: The sensor is unplugged from the correct A-axis or Z-axis connection point without ambiguity.
steps.failure_condition: The connection point cannot be accessed, cannot be identified, or the sensor cannot be unplugged.

actions.label: Disconnect A-axis sensor
actions.exact_action: For an A-axis sensor, unplug the sensor from the printed circuit assembly (PCA) near the A-axis motor.

actions.label: Disconnect Z-axis sensor
actions.exact_action: For a Z-axis sensor, unplug the sensor from the CLINK controller inside the control box.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tip PCA area relevant to the A-axis sensor connection point.
CLINK controller location inside the control box for the Z-axis sensor connection point.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct connection point cannot be identified.
The connection point cannot be accessed.
Disconnecting the sensor would require steps not described in the source.

source_evidence.text: Unplug the sensor from its connection point: A-axis sensors plug into a printed circuit assembly (PCA) near the A-axis motor, and Z-axis sensors plug into the CLINK controller inside the control box.
Figure 7-18 titled "Tip PCA" appears on page 173.
Figure 7-13: CLINK Controller, page 157, identifies the controller or controller box referenced in removal steps.

steps.title: Remove the retaining screw
steps.purpose: Release the sensor from its mounted position.
steps.instruction: Remove the M3 button-head screw holding the sensor in place.
steps.expected_result: The sensor is no longer mechanically fastened at its mounting point.
steps.healthy_condition: The M3 button-head screw is removed and the sensor is free to be withdrawn from the mount.
steps.failure_condition: The retaining screw cannot be removed or the sensor remains fastened.

actions.label: Remove retaining hardware
actions.exact_action: Remove the M3 button-head screw holding the sensor in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Only limited related clamp and small-hardware context is available from this packet; use cautiously as supplemental visual context, not as direct sensor-specific evidence.

stop_or_escalate_if: The M3 button-head screw cannot be removed.
The mounting arrangement does not match the source description.

source_evidence.text: Remove the M3 button-head screw holding the sensor in place. [Image support needed: Maintenance figure showing the flat-flex cable and related clamp hardware used during FFC replacement on the tipper ClearLink controller assembly.]
Figure 7-14 "Flat-Flex Cable" appears on page 161 and shows related clamp hardware context.

steps.title: Feed the sensor through the routing hole during removal
steps.purpose: Free the sensor and cable from the machine's routing path without leaving it snagged in the pass-through.
steps.instruction: Feed the sensor through the hole used for the wire routing as it is removed.
steps.expected_result: The sensor body and cable are moving out through the routing hole.
steps.healthy_condition: The sensor passes through the routing hole and the cable continues to withdraw cleanly.
steps.failure_condition: The sensor or cable binds in the routing hole or cannot be fed through the path described.

actions.label: Withdraw through routing hole
actions.exact_action: Feed the sensor through the hole used for the wire routing as it is removed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cable routing path context that may help verify the withdrawal path.
General tip or A-axis cable context that may help identify the routed cable path.

stop_or_escalate_if: The cable cannot be freed because of routing constraints beyond what is described.
The cable binds and the source provides no additional recovery detail.

source_evidence.text: Feed the sensor through the hole used for the wire routing as it is removed.
If the sensor cable cannot be freed because of routing or wire ties beyond what is described, the source does not provide additional recovery detail.
Figure 7-16, titled "Tip Cable Routing," appears on page 165.
Figure 7-15 "Tip Cable" on page 165.

steps.title: Pull the sensor from the column for Z-axis removal
steps.purpose: Complete physical removal for a Z-axis sensor after it has been disconnected and unfastened.
steps.instruction: For a Z-axis sensor, pull the sensor from the column.
steps.expected_result: The Z-axis sensor is removed from the column.
steps.healthy_condition: The Z-axis sensor and cable withdraw from the column without additional undocumented disassembly.
steps.failure_condition: The Z-axis sensor cannot be pulled from the column.

actions.label: Remove Z-axis sensor
actions.exact_action: For a Z-axis sensor, pull the sensor from the column.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Z-axis sensor does not withdraw from the column.
Additional obstruction or routing detail is encountered that is not covered by the source.

source_evidence.text: For a Z-axis sensor, pull the sensor from the column.

steps.title: Free and remove the A-axis sensor cable
steps.purpose: Complete physical removal for an A-axis sensor and release any simple cable restraint described by the source.
steps.instruction: For an A-axis sensor, pull the defective sensor away. Cut the wire tie if needed to free the cable.
steps.expected_result: The A-axis sensor and cable are freed from the machine routing path.
steps.healthy_condition: The A-axis sensor is removed and any required wire tie has been cut to free the cable.
steps.failure_condition: The cable remains trapped or cannot be freed using the described wire-tie removal.

actions.label: Remove A-axis sensor
actions.exact_action: For an A-axis sensor, pull the defective sensor away.

actions.label: Cut wire tie if needed
actions.exact_action: Cut the wire tie if needed to free the cable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A-axis cable routing context that may help identify where the cable is tied or routed.
Tip PCA area associated with the A-axis sensor cable connection.

stop_or_escalate_if: The cable cannot be freed after cutting the accessible wire tie described by the source.
Routing or restraints beyond the described wire tie prevent removal.

source_evidence.text: For an A-axis sensor, pull the defective sensor away; cut the wire tie if needed to free the cable.
If the sensor cable cannot be freed because of routing or wire ties beyond what is described, the source does not provide additional recovery detail.
Figure 7-16, titled "Tip Cable Routing," appears on page 165.
Figure 7-18 titled "Tip PCA" appears on page 173.

steps.title: Optionally use the old sensor to guide the new cable path
steps.purpose: Preserve the original routing path during replacement when helpful.
steps.instruction: If helpful during replacement, connect the new sensor to the one being replaced before pulling through the cable routing so the new cable follows the same path.
steps.expected_result: If used, the old sensor helps guide the new sensor cable through the same routing path.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement cable can follow the original routing path using the removed sensor as a guide.
steps.failure_condition: This guidance cannot be applied or does not help preserve the routing path.

actions.label: Use old sensor as pull guide
actions.exact_action: If helpful during replacement, connect the new sensor to the one being replaced before pulling through the cable routing so the new cable follows the same path.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Routing context for following the same cable path during replacement.

stop_or_escalate_if: The routing path cannot be preserved using the described guidance.
Replacement requires additional installation detail not present in the source.

source_evidence.text: If helpful during replacement, connect the new sensor to the one being replaced before pulling through the cable routing so the new cable follows the same path.
The note about connecting the new sensor to the old one is included as source-backed handling guidance, even though full installation is not provided.
Figure 7-16, titled "Tip Cable Routing," appears on page 165.

success_criteria: The limit or home sensor is disconnected from the correct connection point.
The M3 button-head retaining screw is removed.
The sensor and cable are removed from the mounting and routing path.
For A-axis removal, any needed wire tie has been cut to free the cable.
For Z-axis removal, the sensor has been pulled from the column.
healthy_conditions: The technician correctly identifies sensor type and axis before removal.
LOTO is applied before any disconnection or hardware removal.
The sensor disconnects from the expected A-axis PCA or Z-axis CLINK controller location.
The cable withdraws through the routing hole without undocumented disassembly.
failure_conditions: LOTO is not applied before removal work.
Sensor type or axis cannot be identified from the available source information.
The correct connection point cannot be identified or accessed.
The M3 button-head screw cannot be removed.
The cable cannot be freed because of routing or wire ties beyond what is described in the source.
escalation_guidance: Stop if LOTO cannot be applied and do not continue removal.
Escalate for SME review if the sensor type, axis, or exact location cannot be confirmed from the available source.
Escalate if cable routing or wire ties prevent removal beyond the limited recovery detail provided in the source.
Escalate if the mounting arrangement or connection point does not match the source description.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: CLINK controller location for Z-axis sensor disconnection.
Tip PCA area for A-axis sensor disconnection.
Cable routing path context for withdrawing and guiding the sensor cable.
General tip or A-axis cable context.
Supplemental small-hardware visual context only; not direct sensor-specific evidence.

The source packet does not provide installation steps for the replacement sensor.
The source packet does not provide estimated time for this specific sensor removal procedure.
The source packet does not provide additional recovery steps if cable routing or wire ties prevent removal.
